title_en: Prayer Law: Al-Dasuqi's Analysis of Maliki Salah
content_en:
Al-Dasuqi's hashiyah on the prayer sections of Al-Sharh al-Kabir addresses some of the most contested and nuanced questions in Maliki salah law. His analysis is particularly valuable where ad-Dardiri's sharh leaves issues underspecified or where there is genuine disagreement within the Maliki school that requires careful navigation.

On the question of intention (niyyah) in prayer, al-Dasuqi elaborates the Maliki school's standard that the intention must be present at the moment of the opening takbir — not before or after it. This requirement of simultaneous intention and action is common across the schools, but the Maliki school is distinctive in emphasizing that the prayer is invalidated if the intention and the takbir are not joined in a single continuous moment of consciousness. Al-Dasuqi clarifies the degree of presence of mind required and discusses what happens if there is a momentary gap between the intention and the verbal beginning of the prayer.

The Maliki school's position on the Friday prayer (jumu'ah) receives careful elaboration in al-Dasuqi's hashiyah. The minimum number required for jumu'ah in the dominant Maliki position is twelve free, resident, adult Muslim men — including the imam. Al-Dasuqi traces the evidence for this number, which is based on a particular reading of the Quranic context (the verse in Surah al-Jumu'ah) and traditions from the Companion Jabir, and compares it with the positions of other schools. He also addresses what happens when the congregation falls below twelve after the prayer has started — a practically important question in small communities.

The two khutbahs (sermons) of jumu'ah are obligatory in the Maliki school. Al-Dasuqi discusses their minimum content requirements: each must include praising God and blessing the Prophet (peace be upon him), at least one must contain an admonition to God-consciousness (taqwa), and one must include Quranic recitation. He also addresses the conditions under which praying behind an imam of a different madhab is valid — an important question in communal Muslim life.

On congregational prayer more broadly, al-Dasuqi addresses the Maliki position on the validity of prayer behind an imam who commits errors. If the imam makes a mistake in recitation, the follower should not correct him aloud — this differs from some other schools' practices. If the imam's prayer is invalidated (for example, because he loses his ablution during the prayer), the Maliki school holds that the followers may complete their prayer individually.

The Maliki school's distinctive position on prayer times is also addressed in the hashiyah. The school holds that the preferred time for 'Isha prayer extends from the disappearance of twilight (shafaq) until the first third of the night, with the permissible time extending to dawn. Al-Dasuqi clarifies what counts as 'twilight' in the Maliki school's calculation — a point of genuine uncertainty in juristic discussions.

The fear prayer (salat al-khawf) — a modified prayer performed in dangerous conditions such as battle — is addressed in detail, with al-Dasuqi explaining the different configurations permitted by the Maliki school and the hadith evidence from which they derive. This section reflects the classical fiqh tradition's thoroughness in addressing even exceptional circumstances.

Al-Dasuqi's hashiyah on prayer law exemplifies his scholarly method: he uses the text of Ad-Dardiri as a springboard for genuine legal analysis, engaging with the diversity of the Maliki tradition and providing students with the analytical tools needed to understand not just what the Maliki school rules but why it rules that way.
content_id:
Hasyiyah al-Dasuqi atas bagian-bagian shalat dari Asy-Syarh al-Kabir membahas beberapa pertanyaan yang paling diperdebatkan dan paling bernuansa dalam hukum shalat Maliki. Analisisnya sangat berharga di mana al-Dardiri meringkas posisi madzhab tanpa memberikan diskusi penuh tentang dasar-dasar atau konteks perdebatan, karena al-Dasuqi mengisi kesenjangan tersebut dengan perlakuan analitis yang lebih luas.

Pada pertanyaan tentang kerasahan berbagai kesalahan dalam shalat — apakah kelalaian memerlukan sujud sahwi, apakah ia membatalkan shalat, atau apakah ia tidak memiliki konsekuensi hukum — al-Dasuqi memberikan diskusi yang sangat terperinci yang mengidentifikasi pandangan-pandangan dalam madzhab dan menilai mana yang lebih kuat. Analisisnya tentang tipologi kesalahan dalam shalat Maliki dan konsekuensi hukumnya adalah salah satu yang paling komprehensif yang tersedia dalam literatur kanonik madzhab.

Bagian-bagian tentang shalat berjamaah, persyaratan imamat, dan kondisi-kondisi di mana seseorang dapat shalat di belakang imam dari madzhab yang berbeda mendapat perhatian cermat dari al-Dasuqi. Perlakuannya terhadap pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini mencerminkan realitas praktis komunitas Muslim Mesir di zamannya, di mana perbedaan madzhab bukanlah abstraksi akademis melainkan tantangan praktis sehari-hari yang harus dihadapi oleh para ulama madzhab Maliki.
title_id: Hukum Shalat: Analisis Al-Dasuqi tentang Shalat Maliki
content_ar:
هذا الجزء من كتاب حاشية الداسوقي يتناول موضوع: الصلاة في حاشية الدسوقي. يُقدّم هذا القسم مادةً علميةً غنيةً مستمدةً من المصادر الإسلامية الأصيلة، تجمع بين الدقة في الاستدلال والوضوح في العرض والشمولية في تناول المسائل.

يعرض المؤلف في هذا الباب خلاصة ما وصل إليه أهل العلم في المسألة المبحوثة، مستنداً إلى الأدلة الشرعية من القرآن الكريم والسنة النبوية وأقوال العلماء المعتمدين عبر القرون.

ومن أبرز ما يتميز به هذا الكتاب: الجمع بين الأصالة العلمية والتيسير على القارئ، بحيث تكون المعلومات في متناول المتخصص والمثقف على حدٍّ سواء. كما يُعنى المؤلف بربط العلوم النظرية بتطبيقاتها العملية في حياة المسلم.

هذا الكتاب في مجمله يُشكّل إضافة نوعية في مكتبة العلوم الإسلامية، ومرجعاً نافعاً لكل باحث في شؤون الإسلام وعلومه.
